item.title: "Being a light at the end of the tunnel": Peer mentor experiences on supporting adolescents with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric psychology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a stigmatized and impairing chronic disease frequently diagnosed during adolescence. Adolescents with IBD may benefit greatly from psychosocial support, with ear...

item.title: Association between coronavirus disease 2019 and new-onset autoimmune diseases during the early phase of the pandemic.
item.description: PloS one - Emerging evidence suggests that viral infections, including SARS-CoV-2, may trigger autoimmunity. This study investigated the risk of developing autoimmune diseases following coronavirus disease 20...

item.title: "She just wants to be a normal kid": Adolescents with inflammatory bowel disease and their caregivers' transition from paediatric to adult care.
item.description: British journal of health psychology - Adolescents with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) experience disruptions to normal adolescent development. Lack of preparation for transition from paediatric to adult care can have negative biopsyc...
